correctness.notes: Theorem 1 / proof of law-sharpness mean-completion identity: The proof uses m_h(P)=E_P[Y1-Y0|D=d_h] as if it were E_P[Y1|D=d_h]. The missing term is E_P[Y0|D=d_h]: consistency gives E_Q[Y1(d_h)|D=d_h]=E_P[Y1|D=d_h]=m_h(P)+E_P[Y0|D=d_h], not m_h(P).; Assumption completion / Theorem 1 finite product-support completion: The theorem claims realization by a finite product-support latent completion preserving P, but the construction preserves the full conditional law of (Y0,Y1)|D=d_h and sets Y1(d_h)=Y1. If P has non-finite observed outcome support, the on-arm potential outcome is not finite-support.

perItemFindings.label: law-sharp
perItemFindings.one_line: [serious] Theorem 1 / proof of law-sharpness mean-completion identity: The proof uses m_h(P)=E_P[Y1-Y0|D=d_h] as if it were E_P[Y1|D=d_h]. The missing term is E_P[Y0|D=d_h]: consistency gives E_Q[Y1(d_h)|D=d_h]=E_P[Y1|D=d_h]=m_h(P)+E_P[Y0|D=d_h], not m_h(P). → The necessity identity for E_Q[Y1(d0)|D=d_h] and the sufficiency check of the diagonal alpha_hh=tau_h(P) are false as written; the proof must replace m_h(P)-tau_h(P) by E_P[Y1|D=d_h]-tau_h(P).

perItemFindings.label: correctness
perItemFindings.one_line: [serious] Assumption completion / Theorem 1 finite product-support completion: The theorem claims realization by a finite product-support latent completion preserving P, but the construction preserves the full conditional law of (Y0,Y1)|D=d_h and sets Y1(d_h)=Y1. If P has non-finite observed outcome support, the on-arm potential outcome is not finite-support. → The law class is ill typed unless P is restricted to finite observed outcome support, or the finite-support claim is weakened to finite off-arm kernels conditional on the observed data.

review.verbatim_critique:
D0.5.1 (math referee) found load-bearing correctness defects — re-derive the flagged step(s):
[serious, conjecture:law-sharp] Theorem 1 / proof of law-sharpness mean-completion identity: The proof uses m_h(P)=E_P[Y1-Y0|D=d_h] as if it were E_P[Y1|D=d_h]. The missing term is E_P[Y0|D=d_h]: consistency gives E_Q[Y1(d_h)|D=d_h]=E_P[Y1|D=d_h]=m_h(P)+E_P[Y0|D=d_h], not m_h(P).. Fix: The necessity identity for E_Q[Y1(d0)|D=d_h] and the sufficiency check of the diagonal alpha_hh=tau_h(P) are false as written; the proof must replace m_h(P)-tau_h(P) by E_P[Y1|D=d_h]-tau_h(P).
[serious, setup] Assumption completion / Theorem 1 finite product-support completion: The theorem claims realization by a finite product-support latent completion preserving P, but the construction preserves the full conditional law of (Y0,Y1)|D=d_h and sets Y1(d_h)=Y1. If P has non-finite observed outcome support, the on-arm potential outcome is not finite-support.. Fix: The law class is ill typed unless P is restricted to finite observed outcome support, or the finite-support claim is weakened to finite off-arm kernels conditional on the observed data.

Referee notes: I reproduced the law-sharp mean-completion argument, the critical-L parametric LP, the relaxed-LP endpoint/frontier containment, and the cross-theorem use of the corrected object. The LP algebra reproduces, but the law-sharp derivation drops the pre-period mean term by treating the level-change mean as a post-period mean, and the finite-support law class is not well typed for a general observable law P.

report_summary: Motivating Conjecture 1 / front-matter law-sharp equality:correctness, Theorem 3 conditional law-sharp coverage cross-reference:correctness

structure.notes: The required Stage 0 sections, locked PartialID parameters, labelled assumptions, and actionable math-object checklist are present; the needed repair is in the setup/front-matter statement, not the skeleton.

novelty.notes: Positioning is targeted to continuous-treatment DiD, shape-restricted PartialID, LP-valued bounds, and in-repository predecessors, with a theorem-level distinction between algebraic envelopes, law-sharp completion, critical frontiers, and relaxed LP inference.

correctness.notes: D0.5.1 reproduced the corrected mean-completion law-sharp theorem but found that the front-matter law-sharp equality still states the uncorrected envelope and omits necessary mean-completion inequalities.

perItemFindings.label: Motivating Conjecture 1 / front-matter law-sharp equality
perItemFindings.one_line: The front-matter equality still claims law-sharpness of the original algebraic envelope [L_h(L;P),U_h(L;P)] even though bounded off-arm support requires the additional inequalities lower y <= beta_h(P)+alpha_jh <= upper y.

perItemFindings.label: Theorem 3 conditional law-sharp coverage cross-reference
perItemFindings.one_line: The proof invokes Conjecture law-sharp as if it yielded Theta_I(P)=[L_h(L;P),U_h(L;P)], but the reproduced corrected result yields the mean-completion interval [L_h^mc(L;P),U_h^mc(L;P)].

review.verbatim_critique: Revise the shared setup and front matter so the law-sharp object is consistently the mean-completion polytope. In the abstract and Introduction's Motivating Conjecture 1, replace the equality with [L_h(L;P),U_h(L;P)] by the corrected [L_h^mc(L;P),U_h^mc(L;P)] statement, or explicitly state that the original algebraic envelope is only an outer algebraic bound and law-sharpness holds after adding the mean-completion inequalities. The required inequalities are lower y <= beta_h(P)+alpha_jh <= upper y for every off-arm j != h, with beta_h(P)=mu_1h(P)-tau_h(P). Also revise the Theorem 3 proof's conditional law-sharp coverage paragraph: it should not assert Theta_I(P)=[L_h(L;P),U_h(L;P)] from the corrected law-sharp theorem. State instead that the corrected law-sharp interval is [L_h^mc(L;P),U_h^mc(L;P)] and that coverage by the relaxed original algebraic interval follows only through the containment [L_h^mc,U_h^mc] subset [L_h,U_h] subset [hat L_h,hat U_h], or update the sample LP to cover the corrected mean-completion endpoints directly.

report_summary: tier=field | The note is structurally complete, adopts a computable PartialID LP envelope with a named mean-completion correction and critical-L frontier, and the pre-adjudicated correctness review passes all load-bearing proof steps.

review.notes: The note is structurally complete, adopts a computable PartialID LP envelope with a named mean-completion correction and critical-L frontier, and the pre-adjudicated correctness review passes all load-bearing proof steps.

structure.notes: All Stage 0 roles are present, the PartialID locked-parameter block is preserved, assumptions are labelled, and the formalization checklist lists the required probabilistic and deterministic LP objects.

novelty.notes: The theorem-level contribution is positioned as a finite-grid continuous-treatment DiD ACRT PartialID envelope with law-sharp mean-completion correction, critical regularity frontier, and relaxed-LP inference, distinct from the cited CGS, shape-restriction, and generic LP-bound literatures.

correctness.notes: D0.5.1 reproduced the mean-completion law-sharpness construction, joint LP envelope, critical-L characterization, and relaxed-LP containment without finding a dropped term or unreproducible step.

journal_recommendations.fit_reasoning: The kernel is an econometric PartialID construction for continuous-treatment DiD with computable LP bounds and inference, but its finite-grid and conditional-envelope scope make it more field-level than flagship-general.
The result fits a methodological audience interested in identification and inference under shape restrictions, especially because the critical regularity frontier is a usable reporting object.
The finite-dimensional LP geometry, law-sharp completion argument, and relaxed projection inference are technically appropriate for an econometric theory venue.

perItemFindings.label: headline
perItemFindings.one_line: Delivered tier subfield < floor field; not salvageable within scope.

review.verbatim_critique: [D0.5.G cold referee] delivered tier=subfield below novelty_target=field (floor=field). The delivered result is a finite-dimensional LP formulation whose main sharpness step becomes a direct two-point mean-completion construction once the missing inequalities are added. The advertised law-sharp interval is sharp only for a very permissive completion class with arbitrary off-arm product kernels, so the causal content is much narrower than the continuous-treatment DiD headline suggests. The critical-L theorem is standard parametric LP basis sensitivity, and the inference theorem gives conservative pointwise outer coverage by enlarging the feasible set with diverging padding rather than a sharp or broadly usable inference theory. This is a real but specialized algebraic partial-identification note, not a field-level contribution.

This is a real but specialized algebraic partial-identification note, not a field-level contribution.
